summary: Purely mechanical report-only PR: updates report.json/README to reflect 23 active real-C functions (across OS, field, battle, TRK, and other units) that were already at 100% match in the current tree but still showed stale sub-100% percentages in report.json. No source files changed.
decomp_lessons: report.json can drift stale relative to the actual current-tree match state -- functions can genuinely re-measure at 100% while the committed report still shows an old lower percentage, requiring periodic 're-gate and bank' sweeps like this one rather than assuming report.json is always live-accurate.

This PR is purely a denominator/numerator bookkeeping fix (no .c changes, no .inc files, no asm wrappers, no pointer-arithmetic additions) -- it's the kind of low-risk 'banking' PR that should be distinguished from PRs claiming new decompilation work.
smart_moves: Grouped a _gate_apply_clean.json re-measure pass across 21+ functions at once rather than banking one at a time, verified via tools/match_scan_file.py plus tools/check_report_sanity.py and tools/verify_commit.py --no-regression-check.

key_files.role: Updated matched_functions/matched_code aggregates to 4,306/6,795 functions (~63.4%) reflecting 23 functions that were already 100% in-tree but stale in the report.
Synced top-level progress table to match the updated report.json numbers.
